I devoted a great deal of my free time to teach myself about networking and programming, developing just enough skills and confidence to eventually change careers and pursue software development for a living. Since 2012 I have earned my stripes as software engineer and for the last 6 years designed, created and maintained Kubernetes based architectures in multi-tenancy environments, crafted a fair share of CI/CD-Pipelines on various different platforms, managed application deployments (leveraging both, best-practices and, in hindsight, not-so-good practices), implemented automations to streamline workflows and overall helped development teams to ship their products faster and reliable, all while keeping a watchful eye on security. I do not hold any degree in CS but I am equipped with unstoppable drive to eventually „figure things out“. Currently I am working as a consultant, guiding and assisting clients to adopt cloud native technologies and to build, maintain and operate cloud (Azure) and hybrid infrastructures.

Maintainer of the cncf Sandbox project, Paralus (https://www.paralus.io/).Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I love sharing my Open Source jourey with others, currently there's a desparate need of mentorship within the community, as cloud native eco-system constantly equipped with new tooling, students, tech pro, difficult to find and map existing knowledge to work with OS and communities, I'm hoping my time as an Ambassador, I'll get on-board and shortened the loop for code to contribution to outreaching new OS tooling to the world.
